input_article.title: Exclusion from rolls doesn’t repeal voting rights forever, says Supreme Court
input_article.summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that rights of voters purged from electoral rolls during the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) exercise cannot be "washed away forever". The court directed the Election Commission of India to provide complete access to reasons for voter exclusion, with appellate tribunals presided over by former Chief Justices and judges. The first phase of West Bengal's Assembly elections will begin on April 23, after a review of approximately 40 lakh claims under adjudication had been completed.

political_articles_data.story_summary: The Supreme Court of India has directed the Election Commission to provide complete access to reasons and remarks recorded by adjudicating officers for voters purged from the electoral roll during the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) exercise. The bench, headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant, said the rights of those excluded cannot be "washed away forever" and assured that justice would be done through appellate hearings before seasoned judges. Around 500 judicial officers are reviewing the decisions taken by Electoral Registration Officers to determine whether voters can remain on the rolls or not. The Supreme Court has also approved the extension of time limits for filing Form 6 to include new voters, amidst the ongoing SIR exercise in West Bengal.

related_reports.title: ‘Happy and optimistic’: Supreme Court lauds Bengal SIR progress as 47 lakh voter objections cleared
related_reports.summary: The Supreme Court expressed satisfaction with the progress of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in West Bengal, which aimed to resolve logical discrepancies. As of now, judicial officers have adjudicated around 47 lakh out of 60 lakh cases and are expected to complete the exercise by April 7. The court also allowed the Election Commission of India's request to extend the time limit for filing Form 6 and approved the constitution of Administrative Tribunals in Bengal.

related_reports.title: 60 lakh Bengal voters in balance: Tall order for deleted electors to return to rolls, say officials - The Indian Express
related_reports.description: 60 lakh Bengal voters in balance: Tall order for deleted electors to return to rolls, say officials  The Indian Express
related_reports.summary:
* The Election Commission of India has identified around 60.06 lakh registered voters in West Bengal who are now in limbo after being marked as "under adjudication" following a Special Intensive Revision (SIR).
* Those identified will have to wait for judicial officers' decisions on their eligibility to remain on the rolls, which may not be as straightforward as filling out a new enrollment form.
* The final electoral roll of West Bengal shows an 8% decrease in electors and another 8% marked as "under adjudication", with the fate of these voters now in the hands of around 500 judicial officers.

input_article.title: Modi chairs Cabinet panel meet on West Asia crisis
input_article.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a special meeting of the Cabinet Committee on Security to review measures taken to address the West Asia conflict. The Cabinet Secretary briefed on efforts to ensure the supply of petroleum products, particularly LNG/LPG, and sufficient power availability. Mr. Modi directed departments to take all possible measures to ameliorate problems faced by citizens and sectors affected by the ongoing global situation.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a special meeting of the Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS) to review measures taken amid the ongoing West Asia crisis. The meeting reviewed efforts to diversify supply sources for LPG and LNG, as well as fuel duty reduction and power sector measures to ensure stability in essential supplies. Efforts are also underway to globally diversify sources of energy, fertilizers, and other supply chains, with new inflows from different countries facilitated.

related_reports.title: PM Modi chairs key security meet amid West Asia crisis; reviews fuel, energy supply
related_reports.description: The government said efforts to globally diversify sources of energy, fertilizers and other supply chains were being taken.
related_reports.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a key meeting of the Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS) to review measures taken by various ministries amid the West Asia crisis. The government has been taking efforts to globally diversify sources of energy, fertilizers, and other supply chains. Initiatives have also been taken to curb hoarding and black marketing, expand piped natural gas connections, and ensure stable power availability during peak summer months.

related_reports.title: PM Modi Holds Key Cabinet Meeting To Discuss West Asia's Evolving Situation; Shipping, Aviation, Energy Discussed - thedailyjagran.com
related_reports.description: PM Modi Holds Key Cabinet Meeting To Discuss West Asia's Evolving Situation; Shipping, Aviation, Energy Discussed  thedailyjagran.com
related_reports.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi held a key meeting of the Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS) on April 1, 2026. The meeting reviewed India's preparedness and potential implications for energy security and supply chains in case of war in the Middle East. The discussion included aspects related to sectors such as energy, agriculture, and logistics, with an emphasis on ensuring the availability of critical supplies like fertilisers and petroleum products.

related_reports.title: PM Modi reviews power, fuel and food security in high-level CCS meeting
related_reports.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a special meeting of the Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS) to review the impact of the Middle East conflict. The government is taking measures to safeguard essential supplies, including diversifying LPG and LNG supply sources, maintaining urea production, and easing power sector norms. Officials have emphasized the need for clear communication to prevent misinformation and rumour mongering amidst the crisis.

input_article.title: Amit Shah asks Manipur CM to accelerate peace process, approves formation of all-woman police battalion
input_article.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah met Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh on April 1, 2026, and asked him to accelerate the ongoing peace process. Shah approved the creation of an all-woman police battalion for the state. The meeting was part of efforts to address law and order issues in Manipur.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah directed Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh on Wednesday to accelerate the ongoing peace process in the state. Shah also approved the creation of an all-woman police battalion and gave in-principle approval for raising a Mahila India Reserve Battalion (IRB) in Manipur. The move comes amid rising tensions over ethnic clashes between the Kuki-Zo and Meitei communities, which have claimed over 260 lives since May 3, 2023.

related_reports.title: Amit Shah asks Manipur CM to speed up peace process
related_reports.description: Union home minister Amit Shah reviews Manipur situation, asks CM Yumnam Khemchand Singh to speed up peace process
related_reports.summary:
Here is a 3-line summary of the news article:

Union Home Minister Amit Shah met with Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh to review the state's law and order situation. Shah directed the CM to accelerate the peace process and consolidate community ties. The meeting came as violence in Manipur continues, claiming over 260 lives and displacing more than 60,000 people since ethnic clashes began on May 3, 2023.

related_reports.title: Amit Shah asks Manipur CM to speed up peace efforts, reviews security situation - India Today NE
related_reports.description: Amit Shah asks Manipur CM to speed up peace efforts, reviews security situation  India Today NE

Shah reviewed the law and order situation in Manipur, assessing the security scenario and asking the government to strengthen community harmony. He also gave in-principle approval for raising a Mahila India Reserve Battalion in the state.

input_article.title: 55,000 households use Census self-enumeration portal on day one
input_article.summary: The Population Census 2027 website went live on April 1, 2026, with around 55,000 households submitting their details through self-enumeration. The online portal will be verified by enumerators during door-to-door surveys starting May 16. The first phase of the census aims to collect information on housing and household amenities from 8 states and union territories.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Around 55,000 households used the self-enumeration portal on the first day of the Population Census 2027 website, which went live in eight States and Union Territories. The online process will be verified by enumerators during door-to-door surveys 15 days later. The exercise marks the first digital Census, with comprehensive information on housing, household amenities, and assets to be collected. The second phase of population enumeration is set for February 2027 for the remaining States.

related_reports.title: Census 2027 begins: President Murmu, PM Modi among leaders completing self-enumeration
related_reports.description: President Droupadi Murmu, Vice President C.P. Radhakrishnan, Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and Union Home Minister Amit Shah have all participated in the self-enumeration for Census 2027. This initiative marks the commencement of the first phase, focusing on house listing and housing census, with a 15-day window for self-enumeration in Delhi starting Wednesday.
related_reports.summary: President Droupadi Murmu participated in the self-enumeration initiative for Census 2027 at Rashtrapati Bhavan. Vice President C P Radhakrishnan also completed his form online, urging residents to participate in the process. Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah have also filled out their respective forms as part of the first phase of Census 2027.

input_article.title: Assembly Election highlights: PM Modi speaks in Assam, Rajnath Singh holds campaign in Kerala, and more
input_article.summary:
Here is a 2-3 line summary of the news article in a neutral tone:

Defence Minister Rajnath Singh criticized the ruling Left Front in Kerala, claiming they betrayed Lord Ayyappa for personal gain. Prime Minister Narendra Modi promised to protect infiltrators if the Congress comes to power in Assam, accusing them of aiming to create a permanent vote bank. Tamil Nadu's M.K. Stalin held an election campaign rally and introduced party candidates for the upcoming Assembly elections.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has accused the Congress of planning to introduce a "dangerous communal law" in Assam if voted to power, targeting the majority community. In contrast, Kerala's ruling Left Front has been criticized by BJP leader Rajnath Singh for allegedly betraying Lord Ayyappa's sentiments over gold loss cases. Meanwhile, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin is contesting elections from Kolathur constituency and addressing public gatherings in Tiruchirappalli district.

related_reports.title: ‘Beware of Congress, it is planning to bring a dangerous law in Assam’: PM Modi
related_reports.description: The PM alleged that the Congress was preparing to introduce a law that would unfairly target the majority community.
related_reports.summary:
* Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused the Congress of planning to introduce a "dangerous communal law" in Assam if voted to power.
* The proposed law, alleged to target the majority community, is reportedly similar to a communal violence law from before 2014 that was opposed by the BJP and NDA allies.
* The Congress had promised to bring such legislation at an election meeting, with plans to impose five years of imprisonment on individuals involved in hate speech against any particular religion or its followers.

related_reports.title: ‘Infiltrator’ vs ‘mafia rule’: PM Modi, Priyanka Gandhi exchange barbs as Assam contest heats up
related_reports.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi made an infiltrator pitch and accused the Congress of being the "root cause of corruption".
related_reports.summary:
* Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi exchanged barbs during a campaign rally in Assam, with both sides accusing each other of corruption and ruling styles.
* Modi accused the Congress of planning to bring a law to protect infiltrators if it comes to power in Assam, while Priyanka Gandhi alleged that the current government is corrupt and operating under "mafia rule".
* The two leaders also spoke about the death of singer Zubeen Garg, with Priyanka Gandhi promising justice to his family within 100 days if Congress wins the assembly election on April 9.

input_article.title: Modi plans FTA push with Europe this summer, fast-tracking implementation on cards
input_article.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi will travel to European countries this summer to discuss trade and technology opportunities with EU officials. The EU's Parliament is set to discuss ratifying the EU-India Free Trade Agreement, which was announced in January. Modi has bilateral visits scheduled with Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, Sweden, and The Netherlands.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi will tie up visits to Nordic countries and Europe this summer as the European Union's Parliament discusses ratifying the EU-India Free Trade Agreement. Modi will attend the G-7 Summit outreach in France, where leaders from the US, Germany, Italy, UK, Japan, Canada, and other countries will meet. The aim of his visits is to discuss trade and technology opportunities ahead of the EU-India FTA implementation, which could boost current trade levels of about $140 billion. Officials hope for early ratification of the agreement in the European Council and Parliament.

related_reports.title: EU leaders back early trade pact, AI cooperation in meetings with Modi - The Times of India
related_reports.description: EU leaders back early trade pact, AI cooperation in meetings with Modi  The Times of India
related_reports.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with leaders from various European countries on Wednesday, including Spain, Finland, Estonia, Croatia, and Serbia, to deepen cooperation on technology, innovation, security, and defense. The meeting coincided with the AI Impact Summit in India, which has seen a record-breaking 26 European countries participating. EU leaders expressed their support for early implementation of the India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) and pledged to work towards its ratification in the European Council and Parliament.

related_reports.description: PM Modi meets EU leaders on AI summit margins | India News  Hindustan Times

The two leaders emphasized cooperation in AI for inclusive development and discussed several bilateral issues, including defence industrial collaboration and the implementation of the India-EU free trade agreement. Strong European participation was noted, with 11 EU countries represented at the summit, highlighting Europe's role as a hub for digital governance and responsible AI frameworks.

input_article.title: Mamata did nothing for Muslims in West Bengal, says Owaisi
input_article.summary:
* AIMIM president Asaduddin Owaisi has started his campaign for the West Bengal Assembly election by addressing a public meeting in Naoda.
* The AIMIM and its ally Aam Janata Unnayan Party (AJUP) plan to contest 182 of the total 294 seats, with Ajup leader Humayun Kabir contesting from the Naoda Assembly constituency.
* Muslims form about 30% of West Bengal's population but make up only around 7% in government jobs.

political_articles_data.story_summary: All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen (AIMIM) president Asaduddin Owaisi has launched his campaign for the West Bengal Assembly election with an alliance partner, Aam Janata Unnayan Party (AJUP) leader Humayun Kabir. The alliance plans to contest 182 of the total 294 seats and is expected to carve out space in a contest largely dominated by the BJP and the TMC. Muslims form about 30% of West Bengal's population but hold only seven per cent of government jobs, with Owaisi accusing Trinamool Congress chairperson Mamata Banerjee of doing nothing for the community. The AIMIM and AJUP alliance aims to bring together voices representing marginalized communities.

related_reports.title: ‘Alliance to continue in 2029 Lok Sabha polls’: Owaisi and Humayun Kabir join forces in Murshidabad
related_reports.summary:
Ravik Bhattacharya is the Chief of Bureau for The Indian Express, Kolkata, with over 20 years of experience in journalism. He has reported extensively across West Bengal, Odisha, Assam, and the Andaman Nicobar Islands, covering politics, crime, and investigative stories. Ravik Bhattacharya won the Ramnath Goenka Excellence in Journalism Award in 2007 for his political reporting.

Atri Mitra is a Special Correspondent for The Indian Express, with over 20 years of experience in reporting on administration and political news from West Bengal, Bihar, and the North-East. He holds a Master's degree in Economics from Rabindrabharati University and has reported on several major elections, including the 2009 Lok Sabha election and the 2019 Bihar Lok Sabha election.

A new alliance has been formed between Humayun Kabir's Aam Janata Unnayan Party (AJUP) and Asaduddin Owaisi's AIMIM ahead of West Bengal's Assembly elections. The alliance plans to contest 190 out of 294 seats in the state, with both leaders launching their campaign from Murshidabad district starting April 1. This development is seen as a potential realignment in the Muslim vote bank, which has traditionally been crucial for Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress.

input_article.title: Hope for Bengal electors pending under adjudication, but uncertainty over redressal at tribunals
input_article.summary: The Chief Justice of the Calcutta High Court informed the Supreme Court that nearly 47 lakh out of 60 lakh claims under adjudication had already been disposed of. The remaining claims are expected to be resolved by April 7. The number of voters whose names have been deleted from the electoral roll is approximately 87 lakh, which could potentially bring the total voter list below 70 crore.

political_articles_data.story_summary: The Supreme Court has informed that judicial officers have disposed of nearly 47 lakh out of 60 lakh claims under adjudication during the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) in West Bengal, with all objections expected to be decided by April 7. The bench also noted that electors whose names are struck off can approach appellate tribunals presided over by former Chief Justices and judges of High Courts. With over 80 lakh deletions, the electoral roll is likely to fall below 7 crore voters before polling, scheduled in two phases on April 23 and 29.

The Supreme Court has instructed that appellate tribunals must have access to reasons recorded by adjudicating officers for voter exclusion and will review further on April 6. A total of 1.75-2 lakh objections are being dealt with daily, according to the Calcutta High Court Chief Justice.

related_reports.title: ‘Happy and optimistic’: Supreme Court lauds Bengal SIR progress as 47 lakh voter objections cleared

The court rejected objections to the Election Commission's extension of time limits for filing forms and approved the training provided to presiding officers of Administrative Tribunals (ATs). The ATs are expected to complete the exercise by April 7.

input_article.title: Modi America’s slave, has put country in crisis: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra
input_article.summary: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of becoming a "slave" to the United States and pushing the country into crisis. She also criticized Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma for allegedly being involved in various scams and serving his own interests over those of the people. The single-phase election for Assam's 126-member Assembly is scheduled for April 9, with counting of votes set for May 4.

political_articles_data.story_summary:
Here's a unified news summary combining all sources:

Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra has launched a scathing attack on the BJP-led government in Assam, alleging that it wants to establish a "double Ghulami govt" and reduce the people of the state to slavery. She accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of acting as a "slave" of the United States and pushing India towards a crisis. Meanwhile, Modi has promised a third consecutive NDA win and implementation of a Uniform Civil Code in Assam, claiming that Congress is planning to bring a "dangerous communal law" that would unfairly target the majority community. The allegations come ahead of the upcoming assembly elections in Assam.

input_article.title: H.S. Phoolka, who fought for anti-Sikh riot victims, joins BJP
input_article.summary: Senior advocate H.S. Phoolka joined the Bharatiya Janata Party on April 1, 2026, in the presence of Hardeep Singh Puri. Prior to this, Phoolka had previously joined the Aam Aadmi Party and the Shiromani Akali Dal before quitting politics. He was awarded the Padma Shri in 2019 for his legal crusade against 1984 anti-Sikh riots victims.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Senior advocate H.S. Phoolka, known as "messiah" for his long-standing fight against the 1984 anti-Sikh riots victims, joined the Bharatiya Janata Party on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, ahead of the 2027 Punjab assembly polls. Phoolka, a former Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) MLA and Padma Shri recipient, has been fighting for justice for the victims pro bono for over three decades, supported by the BJP since its inception. His decision to join the party comes after a six-year hiatus from politics.

related_reports.title: Lawyer for 1984 riot victims, ex-AAP MLA HS Phoolka joins BJP

Phoolka has been fighting for victims of the 1984 anti-Sikh riots and previously pursued a case against Congress leaders Sajjan Kumar and Jagdish Tytler. He was awarded the Padma Shri in 2019 and had previously served as Leader of Opposition in the Punjab Assembly.

related_reports.title: 1984 anti-Sikh riots survivor, ‘crusader for other victims’, Vajpayee admirer: Who is HS Phoolka, who joined BJP
related_reports.summary: Harvinder Singh Phoolka, a senior Supreme Court Advocate and human rights activist, has joined the Bharatiya Janata Party ahead of the 2027 Punjab assembly polls. Phoolka, who was known for fighting cases of 1984 anti-Sikh riot victims pro bono, had previously been a member of the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) from 2014 to 2019. He will now work in politics to support Punjab as a BJP member.

related_reports.title: ‘PM Modi likes Punjabis, and me too’: Joining BJP, HS Phoolka recalls 1984 trauma, targets Congress and AAP
related_reports.summary: Harvinder Singh Phoolka, a senior Supreme Court advocate and human rights activist, joined the BJP in Delhi on Wednesday. He invoked his decades-long association with the party and the 1984 anti-Sikh riots cases, recalling his personal trauma and launching attacks on the Congress and AAP governments in Punjab. Phoolka's decision to join AAP in 2014 was reportedly a "mistake", according to himself.

input_article.title: Government skips FCRA amendment Bill; Rijiju accuses Opposition of spreading ‘wrong things’ before Kerala polls
input_article.summary: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju announced that the government would not bring a Bill to amend the Foreign Contributions (Regulation) Act (FCRA) during the ongoing Budget session. The Opposition had been protesting against the provisions of the FCRA (Amendment) Bill, which aims to protect national security and interest by preventing misuse of foreign contributions. The bill's introduction has cast a shadow over the BJP's outreach to the Christian community in Kerala ahead of the state's Assembly elections.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju on Wednesday informed Lok Sabha that the government will not bring a Bill to amend the Foreign Contributions (Regulation) Act (FCRA) in the ongoing Budget session, amid strong protests from Opposition parties and concerns raised by Christian organisations and BJP leaders. The Bill had been tabled last month, but its introduction was put on hold due to pressure from Kerala's BJP unit and criticism of its provisions being misused against minority institutions. The move has sparked opposition from Congress and Left MPs, who accused the government of misleading the House for the sake of elections.

related_reports.title: Christians, Kerala parties opposed, Govt drops new FCRA Bill debate
related_reports.summary: The Indian government dropped the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026 from its legislative business agenda due to opposition from Congress and Left parties. The bill aimed to amend the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act, 2010 and introduce a "comprehensive statutory framework" for managing foreign contributions and assets. Christian organizations and some BJP leaders in Kerala had expressed concerns over the bill's provisions, citing potential risks to minority institutions and NGOs.

input_article.title: Lok Sabha passes Bill recognising Amaravati as sole capital of Andhra Pradesh
input_article.summary:
* The Lok Sabha has passed a Bill to recognize Amaravati as the sole and permanent capital of Andhra Pradesh.
* The YSR Congress Party walked out in protest after the bill was passed, citing concerns over the interests of farmers who lost land for the capital project.
* The bill will now be sent to the Rajya Sabha and then to the President for assent, which would give statutory backing to Amaravati as the State's capital effective from June 2, 2024.

political_articles_data.story_summary: The Lok Sabha has passed a bill making Amaravati the sole and permanent capital of Andhra Pradesh, with the government seeking to formally designate the city through the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation (Amendment) Bill, 2026. The bill received support from both treasury and opposition benches, but some lawmakers from the YSR Congress Party staged a walkout in protest. If enacted, it will give statutory backing to Amaravati as the State's capital with effect from June 2, 2024, effectively foreclosing any future attempt to alter the decision.

related_reports.title: Bill to declare Amaravati as Andhra Pradesh's capital likely in Lok Sabha today
related_reports.description: Union home minister Amit Shah will introduce a bill to formally designate Amaravati as Andhra Pradesh's capital, amending the 2014 Reorganisation Act.
related_reports.summary: The Union Home Minister Amit Shah will introduce a bill in Parliament to formally designate Amaravati as Andhra Pradesh's capital. The bill aims to amend the 2014 Reorganisation Act, which designated Hyderabad as the common capital of Telangana and Andhra Pradesh for a period not exceeding 10 years. If passed by Parliament, the amendment would mark a major legislative step in reinforcing Amaravati's status as the sole capital of Andhra Pradesh.

related_reports.title: Lok Sabha clears Bill to make Amaravati sole capital of Andhra Pradesh, YSR Congress stages walkout
related_reports.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation (Amendment) Bill, 2026 by a voice vote, recognizing Amaravati as the new capital of Andhra Pradesh with effect from June 2, 2024. The Bill was introduced by Union MoS, Home Affairs, Nityanand Rai, following a resolution passed by the Andhra Pradesh Assembly on March 28, which urged the Centre to incorporate Amaravati as the new capital. The YSR Congress Party opposed the Bill in its present form, citing concerns over financial interests and land compensation for affected farmers.

input_article.title: Rajya Sabha clears Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code Amendments
input_article.summary: The Rajya Sabha has passed the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (Amendment) Bill 2026. Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman attributed the law's success to its ability to strengthen the banking system and increase investor confidence. The bill aims to improve transparency in the recovery process, with steps being taken to prevent frivolous proceedings.

political_articles_data.story_summary: India's Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) Amendment Bill was approved by the Lok Sabha on Monday, allowing for fast-track debt resolution without unseating defaulting management. The bill aims to improve investor confidence and maximise value in the economy. Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that IBC has strengthened the banking system and helped recover ₹54,528 crore of non-performing assets. The amendments are expected to enhance transparency and corporate governance practices in companies undergoing insolvency resolution.

The amendments were introduced by Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman as part of the IBC (Amendment) Bill, 2025.

input_article.title: ATF price hike: IndiGo recalibrates fuel surcharge for domestic flights; steep hike in levy on international flights
input_article.summary: The Indian government has revised fuel surcharges for domestic flights, replacing the flat fee with a distance-based tiered structure. For international flights, surcharges have been increased significantly, ranging from Rs 900 to Rs 10,000 depending on destination and flight distance. The new prices reflect a partial increase in jet fuel costs, which had more than doubled in the last month due to surge in ATF prices.

political_articles_data.story_summary:
Here's a unified news summary combining all sources:

India's scheduled airlines will face higher fuel surcharges for domestic flights, with prices ranging from Rs 275 to Rs 950. For international flights, prices have been increased from Rs 425-2,300 to Rs 900-10,000. The government has allowed only a fraction of the actual price increase to be passed on to airlines. Despite a significant surge in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices, domestic airlines will pay reduced rates compared to international routes.

related_reports.summary: The Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) and other state-run oil marketing firms have increased the price of commercial liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cylinders by over 10% in major metro cities, starting April 1, 2026. The price of aviation turbine fuel (ATF) for international air travel and chartered flights was more than doubled to a record ₹2.07 lakh per kilolitre. ATF rates for scheduled domestic carriers were increased by less than 9%.

related_reports.description: Aviation turbine fuel prices for domestic airlines rose from  ₹96,638.14 per kilolitre to  ₹207,341.22 per kilolitre in Delhi, registering a 114.55% increase.
related_reports.summary:
* India's commercial LPG rate has been increased by ₹195.50 to ₹2,078.50 per 19kg cylinder in Delhi, marking a 10.4% rise.
* Aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices for domestic airlines have risen by 114.55%, from ₹96,638.14 per kilolitre to ₹207,341.22 per kilolitre in Delhi.
* The Indian government has intervened and partially rolled back the ATF price increase, allowing scheduled domestic airlines to pay a lower rate of ₹1,04,927 per kilolitre.

input_article.summary: The BJP and Trinamool Congress (TMC) have been accused of using vote-bank politics to polarize Hindu-Muslim relations in West Bengal. input_article.title: Indian Railways’ boarding station change rules 2026 get effective: Eligibility and step-by-step guide
input_article.summary: Indian Railways' new boarding station change rules 2026 have come into effect, allowing passengers with confirmed or RAC tickets to modify their boarding point up to 30 minutes before scheduled departure. Passengers can now digitally change their boarding location through IRCTC online portal or by visiting a PRS counter at a railway station. The changes are subject to the condition that the request must be submitted before the second chart is prepared, reflecting on the new rules for better onboard management and seat utilization.

The rule aims to enhance flexibility for travellers, especially in major cities with multiple stations. Passengers with confirmed and RAC (Reservation Against Cancellation) tickets can make changes under specific conditions. This change is part of the Ministry of Railways' "52 Reforms in 52 Weeks" initiative to improve efficiency and service delivery.

input_article.title: Maharashtra announces authority for river rejuvenation
input_article.summary: The Maharashtra State River Rejuvenation Authority was approved by the cabinet, which will oversee rejuvenation projects for 54 polluted river stretches in the state. The authority's Chairperson will be the Chief Minister, with the Environment and Climate Change Minister serving as Vice-Chairperson. The authority aims to reduce pollution and conserve rivers through regulatory and developmental work, including determining priority phases for rehabilitation and implementing management plans.

political_articles_data.story_summary: The Maharashtra Cabinet has approved the establishment of the Maharashtra State River Rejuvenation Authority to tackle river pollution and restore 54 polluted river stretches across the state. The authority, led by the Chief Minister, will oversee conservation projects, sewage treatment, and pollution control, with an initial allocation of Rs 400 crore per year from the Environment and Climate Change Ministry's annual budget over five years. The state aims to address pollution through a coordinated multi-departmental approach, focusing on priority rivers including Godavari, Bhima (Chandrabhaga), Indrayani, Mula, Mutha, and Nag.

related_reports.summary: The Maharashtra State River Rejuvenation Authority (MSRRA) will be an apex body tackling river pollution and undertaking basin-level rejuvenation, with the Environment Ministry seeking Rs 400 crore annually for its operation over five years. The authority aims to address pollution in 54 polluted river stretches in Maharashtra, which has the highest number of polluted rivers among states. The MSRRA will function as a coordinated, multi-departmental approach, integrating regulatory and developmental roles to tackle river pollution.

input_article.title: Christians, Kerala parties opposed, Govt drops new FCRA Bill debate
input_article.summary: The Indian government's Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill was dropped from consideration due to opposition from Congress and Left parties. The Bill aimed to amend the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act, 2010, which regulates foreign contributions to NGOs and similar associations. The bill's provisions were criticized by Christian organizations and BJP leaders in Kerala for potentially allowing government control over minority institutions' assets.

political_articles_data.story_summary: India's government has dropped the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026 from its legislative agenda amid strong opposition from parties and Christian organizations. The bill aimed to amend the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act, 2010 but faced criticism that it could target Christian groups in poll-bound Kerala. Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju cited opposition protests as reason for deferring debate on the bill. The move comes after intense pushback from Opposition parties and concerns raised by Christian organizations.

related_reports.title: Govt defers debate on FCRA bill after stir by Oppn, row in Kerala
related_reports.description: The government postponed the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill amid Opposition protests, fearing it could target Christian groups in Kerala.
related_reports.summary:
* The Indian government postponed the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill in the Lok Sabha amid Opposition protests over concerns it could target Christian groups in Kerala.
* The bill, which aims to regulate foreign contributions to non-governmental organizations, had a provision that sparked controversy, allowing for the seizure of assets created from foreign funds by an NGO whose licence was cancelled or not renewed.
* Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju told lawmakers on Wednesday that the government was not shelving the bill but would consider introducing it later, after the current session of Parliament.

Yadav alleged that capable officers are not valued in the current system and are facing growing dissatisfaction due to various instances of non-cooperation from officials.

Azad Samaj Party chief Chandrashekhar Azad also condemned the situation, stating that Rahi's resignation raises serious questions about the entire administrative system. The order, which declared that children born to parents who are in the US illegally or temporarily are not American citizens, has not taken effect anywhere in the US and was challenged by a lower court. A decision is expected by the end of June.

Conservative and liberal justices questioned whether the order comports with the Constitution or federal law, sounding skeptical about the White House's argument. The court is expected to rule by early summer, potentially reversing over a century of unbroken legal precedent on birthright citizenship. The case frames another test of Trump's assertions of executive power.

input_article.title: Odisha at 90: How a movement to unify Odia speakers created India’s first linguistic province
input_article.summary: Odisha celebrated its 90th Utkal Divas on April 1, 1936, marking the formation of the state as the first to be formed on a linguistic identity. The state's formation was led by the Utkal Sammilani movement, championed by Madhusudan Das, who united leaders fighting for Odia nationalism under one platform. The British authorities ultimately established Odisha as a separate province in 1936 after decades of efforts by Odia nationalists, including prominent figures such as Gopabandhu Das and Maharaja Krushna Chandra Gajapati.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Odisha, India's first state formed on linguistic identity, celebrates its 90th Utkal Divas today. The state's history dates back to the late 19th century when a movement sought unification of Odia-speaking regions under British rule. Led by Madhusudan Das and other prominent leaders, the Utkal Sammilani championed the cause, leading to the formation of Odisha as a separate province in 1936. President Droupadi Murmu has praised the state's rich cultural heritage and resilience of its people.

* Utkal Divas was celebrated across Odisha on April 1 to mark the formation of the state as a separate entity on linguistic basis in 1936.

input_article.title: Former Raymond group chief Vijaypat Singhania passes away
input_article.summary: Vijaypat Kailashpat Singhania, former chairman of the Raymond group, died on Saturday at the age of 87. Singhania held multiple Guinness World Records and was also known for his passion for aviation and adventure.

His legacy will continue to guide and inspire generations, according to his son Gautam Singhania. A funeral assembly will be held on Sunday at Chandanwadi crematorium.

input_article.title: New US visa rules from March 30 expand screening for H-1B applicants
input_article.summary: The US State Department has announced that it will expand its screening process for visa applicants to include a broader review of their online presence, starting from March 30. The new rules will apply to a wide range of non-immigrant visa categories, including H-1B visas and family-based visas such as fiancé(e) and spouse categories. The expanded review will also cover cultural and religious visas, as well as special categories such as informants and victims of crime or trafficking.

political_articles_data.story_summary: The US Department of State announced that it will expand its screening process for visa applicants starting March 30, requiring a broader review of their online presence and mandating public access to social media profiles. The new rules will apply to a wider range of non-immigrant visa categories, including H-1B visas, H-4 dependents, and family-based visas such as K visas. Applicants are instructed to adjust the privacy settings on all of their social media profiles to "public" to facilitate this vetting. The move aims to address abuse of the H-1B program while permitting companies to hire the best temporary foreign workers.

The Speaker of the assembly proposed establishing a House Committee to question officials involved in the incident and place its report before the House.

The incident has also raised questions about the effectiveness of security arrangements at high-profile events, including a recent wedding function where Abdullah was targeted. The J&K government is under pressure to explain its response to the attack.

The police have registered a case under the Arms Act and seized the weapon, which was licensed by Jain. The incident has sparked criticism from opposition parties, who claim it reflects the "state of lawlessness" in Odisha under BJP rule. Jain's PSO has been suspended and is being summoned for allegedly assisting him with gun handling.

input_article.title: UPSC Key: Major relief for oil PSUs, Sex vs gender, and Fall in gold prices
input_article.summary:
Here is a 3-line summary of the news article in a neutral tone:

The Indian government has reduced excise duty on petrol and diesel, lowering the levy on petrol from Rs 21.9 per litre to Rs 11.9 per litre, and on diesel from Rs 17.8 per litre to Rs 7.8 per litre. The move aims to alleviate inflationary pressure caused by high international fuel prices. The government has assured that India's strategic petroleum and LPG reserves are sufficient for at least the next 60 days of consumption.

political_articles_data.story_summary:
Here's a unified news summary in 3-4 lines combining all sources:

India has reduced excise duty on petrol and diesel amid rising global oil prices due to the US-Iran war in West Asia. The move aims to ease financial strain on state-run oil retailers and ensure domestic fuel stocks, but will result in estimated revenue loss of ₹1.70 lakh crore if it lasts a year. Despite falling global crude rates, domestic fuel prices remain unlikely to drop significantly due to taxation and import dependence. The government has also tied up crude oil procurement for the next two months to cover 60 days of consumption.

related_reports.title: India slashes additional excise duty on petrol, diesel amid fuel price hike worries — What does this mean for you? - Mint
related_reports.description: India slashes additional excise duty on petrol, diesel amid fuel price hike worries — What does this mean for you?  Mint
related_reports.summary: The Indian government has reduced the special additional excise duty on petrol and diesel to ₹3 per litre from ₹13 per litre and ₹0 from ₹10 per litre, respectively. The move aims to provide relief to oil marketing companies as crude oil prices continue to trade above $100 per barrel amid the US-Iran war in the Middle East. The reduction is expected to reduce the Centre's excise duty revenue collection but protect state-run oil retailers' books from the impact of a potential fuel price hike.

related_reports.title: Govt cuts excise on petrol, diesel by  ₹10 per litre; slaps levies on exports
related_reports.description: The dual measure is aimed at easing the financial strain on state-run oil retailers and ensuring adequate domestic stocks.

The move is expected to lead to an estimated revenue loss of ₹1.70 lakh crore if it lasts the full financial year. The government has also imposed levies on exports of diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF).

related_reports.title: From office to dark streets: How oil crunch is reshaping daily life in different nations
related_reports.description: Global economies reel as Middle East conflict escalates, disrupting oil supplies via the Strait of Hormuz. Nations like Vietnam and India are cutting fuel taxes, while Japan and Germany consider increased coal use. Venezuela faces power cuts, and Pakistan grapples with soaring jet fuel prices. Sri Lanka implements strict fuel rationing, highlighting widespread energy challenges.
related_reports.summary: The Middle East conflict has led to a global energy supply crisis, tightening Tehran's grip on the Strait of Hormuz and disrupting global seaborne trade. Vietnam has lowered fuel prices in response, while India has reduced excise duty on petrol and diesel to cushion consumers. Pakistan is adjusting fuel prices selectively, with kerosene rates increased by PKR 4.66 per litre.

related_reports.title: Strait of Hormuz chokehold: What is happening to petrol prices in India and neighbouring nations amid oil supply crunch?
related_reports.description: Global crude markets are strained as Middle East conflict escalates, pushing oil prices up and impacting fuel supplies worldwide. Countries like India, Nepal, Bhutan, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Myanmar, and Bangladesh are implementing measures such as price adjustments, import increases, and conservation efforts to manage the crisis.
related_reports.summary: Global crude markets remain under strain due to rising prices, nearly 50% since US and Israel launched strikes on Iran. India, heavily reliant on Middle Eastern oil imports, has lowered excise duty on petrol and diesel to cushion consumers and oil companies from price increases. Nepal, Sri Lanka, Bhutan, and Pakistan have also introduced measures to deal with the supply crisis, including fuel price hikes and conservation efforts.

input_article.title: Amaravati as Andhra’s ‘only capital’: Naidu’s new assembly resolution aims to make it ‘immovable’ and beyond legal challenge
input_article.summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu has moved a resolution in the Legislative Assembly to grant statutory recognition to Amaravati as the state's official capital. The resolution aims to make Amaravati "immovable" and beyond legal challenge through amendments to the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Act, 2014. Amaravati was envisioned by Naidu as a world-class capital akin to Hyderabad, Bengaluru, and Chennai, with a focus on rapid development and decentralised growth.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu has moved a key resolution in the Legislative Assembly urging the Union Government to grant statutory recognition to Amaravati as the state's official capital. The resolution, passed unanimously by the Assembly, aims to define Amaravati's boundaries and provide legal certainty on its status. If approved, it would make Amaravati "immovable" and beyond a legal challenge. The move is seen as a significant step towards clarity on the capital issue.

Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu said the purpose of the resolution was to get official recognition and define boundaries for comprehensive development. The resolution will be sent to the Centre immediately, with the aim of giving Amaravati permanent capital status by 2028.

input_article.title: MEA clears air on NYT report: ‘Telephone conversation on March 24 was between PM Narendra Modi and President Donald Trump only’
input_article.summary:
US President Donald Trump had a phone call with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on March 24, which was confirmed by the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) as being between only the two leaders.

The call reportedly discussed the escalating crisis in the Middle East, specifically the Iranian military's control of the Strait of Hormuz and its impact on global energy markets.

Elon Musk, billionaire businessman and owner of SpaceX and Tesla, allegedly participated in the call without his knowledge or consent.

political_articles_data.story_summary: India has clarified that billionaire Elon Musk did not participate in a phone call between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump on March 24. According to the Ministry of External Affairs, the telephone conversation was only between the two leaders, discussing the West Asia conflict. The government had previously stated that the conversation provided an opportunity for exchange of views on the situation in West Asia, but did not deny or confirm Musk's presence during the call, which is unusual for a private citizen during a crisis.

Official spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal stated that the conversation was only between Trump and Modi, without specifying whether Musk participated.

Banerjee also criticized the party's alleged intolerance towards Bengali-speaking people, claiming they are attacked in other states and the ruling party at the Centre grabs power by inciting riots. She vowed to not spare rioters and urged people to forgive her if she committed a sin, while also vowing to rebuild shops destroyed in Behala.

The current daily requirement of LPG for autos is 202 Metric Tonnes, but only 147 MT is supplied, leaving a deficit of approximately 55 MT. The shortage has been triggered by two to three day delays in the arrival of imported LPG due to geopolitical tensions affecting global supply chains.

Meanwhile, in Maharashtra, Food and Civil Supplies minister Chhagan Bhujbal has urged consumers with piped gas availability to apply for pipeline gas connections before June 30, warning that disconnection may occur if not applied for within this timeframe. India aims to secure 3,07,000 MW of thermal-coal and lignite-based capacity by 2034-35 to meet projected electricity demand.

input_article.title: Centre seeks greater control over content posted by independent news creators
input_article.summary: The Indian government has proposed draft amendments to its Information Technology rules, which could allow the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (MIB) to issue direct blocking orders against content posted by independent news creators on platforms such as YouTube and Instagram. The proposed changes would grant the MIB more control over online speech and require social media companies to comply with government advisories. Comments on the draft amendments are due by April 14, and the move is likely to raise concerns over the government's increasing control on online speech.

The move aims to tighten control over online speech and has raised concerns about censorship and regulatory power. Proposed amendments to the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021, have been invited for comments until April 14. The regulations would grant the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting greater authority to regulate online content.

Several Opposition parties have urged the government to send the bill to a select committee for consultation with stakeholders.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Opposition MPs in India's Rajya Sabha have criticized the Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill, 2026, calling it unconstitutional and unfair. Opponents claim that the bill would affect morale among CAPF personnel and ask for it to be sent to a select committee for examination. The government had dismissed a contempt petition last year over its failure to reduce posts earmarked for deputation in CAPFs as per a 2025 Supreme Court order.

input_article.title: Govt says adequate fertilizer stocks in India ahead of Kharif season; costly LNG from spot market could raise subsidy burden
input_article.summary: India's overall fertilizer requirement for the upcoming Kharif season is estimated at 390 lakh tonnes, a 29% increase from last year's actual sales of 361 lakh tonnes. The country currently has sufficient stocks, with approximately 180 lakh tonnes in storage, and efforts are underway to diversify sourcing to reduce dependence on West Asia. Fertilizer subsidy for the current fiscal year is estimated at ₹1.86 lakh crore, up from the original budget estimate due to increased global prices of inputs like LNG.

input_article.title: ‘Selfhood not concession’: Rajasthan High Court grants 3% weightage to transgender persons, slams 2026 Bill for ‘diluting’ rights
input_article.summary: The Rajasthan High Court expressed concerns over the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, which proposes to remove the right to self-perceived gender identity. The court stated that "selfhood is not a matter of concession, but a matter of right" and urged the state to preserve constitutional guarantees. The court also directed that transgender persons should be granted 3% additional weightage in selection and appointment processes until a constitutionally compliant reservation framework is formulated.

The amendment risks undermining the Supreme Court's recognition of transgender individuals as a fundamental aspect of personhood. input_article.title: Recognition without protection: The flaw in India’s anti-discrimination logic
input_article.summary: India's anti-discrimination laws focus heavily on identity recognition, which can lead to unease and concerns about exclusion or misuse. The country has seen debates over amendments to laws protecting Dalit Christians and transgender persons, highlighting a common issue with defining discrimination through identity categories. A mismatch exists between the law's reliance on identity and the often-messy nature of actual discriminatory practices, risking both excluded cases and suspicion about included ones.

political_articles_data.story_summary: India's anti-discrimination laws face criticism over their reliance on identity recognition, which can lead to confusion and exclusion of marginalized groups. The recent amendment to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act has sparked concerns about the definition of a "transgender person," with some critics arguing that it excludes certain identities. Additionally, the proposed anti-discrimination regulations by the University Grants Commission have raised questions about how identity is operationalized and whether it can lead to false or exaggerated complaints. The issue is further complicated by debates over caste and gender laws, including the exclusion of Dalit Christians from Scheduled Caste status and the passage of the Karnataka Freedom of Choice in Marriage Bill, which provides legal protection for couples in inter-caste relationships.

related_reports.title: HistoriCity | Transgender identities always a part of India’s cultural discourse
related_reports.description: In ancient times, members of the transgender community were often integrated into religious and community life
related_reports.summary: India's Parliament has passed an amendment to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019, which replaces its broad definition of a "transgender person" with a narrower formulation. The amendment specifically recognizes certain socio-cultural identities such as hijra, kinner, aravani, and jogta, but excludes other identities. Ancient Indian texts, including the Kama Sutra and Manusmriti, refer to gender diversity, acknowledging "third genders" such as pums-prakriti (male), stri-prakriti (female), and Tritiya Prakriti (third gender).

The bill aims to provide legal protection for couples in inter-caste relationships who face threats, violence or coercion from anyone. The bill proposes penalties, including a minimum five-year prison sentence for killings in the name of "honour", and provides institutional support for couples facing ostracism or violence.

political_articles_data.story_summary: The Indian government has passed the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, which has been criticized by experts for not consulting the National Council for Transgender Persons (NCTP), a statutory body advising on policies and programs for the transgender community. NCTP expert member Dr Aparna Lalingkar claimed that the new definition in the bill forces people to undergo gender-affirming surgery for recognition of their identity, which is unnecessary. The bill was passed by both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha with some opposition demands being met, but critics argue that it may have an impact on the dignity of third-gender people.

As per the state's surrender and rehabilitation policy, Rao will receive a reward of Rs 25 lakh along with other benefits.

The move comes ahead of the Centre's deadline to end the Maoist movement in India (March 31). Rao, who worked for about 36 years with the CPI(Maoist), surrendered citing the decline of the movement and loss of public support, and will receive a reward of Rs 25 lakh as per the state's surrender and rehabilitation policy. The police seized a cache of 19 weapons from the surrendered Maoists.

The ruling party claims these transfers are causing problems in the administration's working, while officials from both the state and Centre dispute this, stating that administrative reshuffles during election periods are normal.

The government has implemented measures to manage the shortage, including rationing and increasing fuel prices, and has sought emergency aid from countries like India. Sri Lanka's economy is vulnerable to external shocks, particularly those affecting energy supplies, and long-term structural reforms are necessary to address such crises in the future.

The fuel crisis is the latest strain on a nation already reeling from years of conflict, economic downturns, and natural disasters. Sri Lanka's reliance on external energy sources has left it vulnerable to global developments.

input_article.summary:
* Former Indian Foreign Secretary Nirupama Menon Rao and former Pakistani Foreign Minister Hina Rabbani Khar recently engaged in a conversation about India-Pakistan relations on social media.
* The exchange began when Rao criticized India's approach to the US-Iran war, stating that "strategic autonomy" cannot mean adjusting one's language to the hierarchy of power.
* Khar responded by framing the issues raised by the US-Iran war as areas where India and Pakistan should work together, and suggested the need for women to take a more active role in shaping their countries' foreign policies.

political_articles_data.story_summary:
Here's a unified news summary in 3-4 lines:

Former Indian foreign secretary Nirupama Menon Rao and former Pakistani foreign minister Hina Rabbani Khar engaged in a rare conversation about finding common ground with Pakistan amid the ongoing US-Iran war, suggesting that more women should be involved in decision-making. The exchange highlighted the need for diplomacy and restraint in international relations. Experts like Salman Khurshid have also expressed concerns about the conflict's lasting impact on global stability and the economy. India's foreign policy has been impacted by the war, with rising costs and limited diplomatic room.

input_article.title: Chhattisgarh: Sai launches Alliance Air flights from Ambikapur to Delhi, Kolkata
input_article.summary: The Government of Chhattisgarh has launched Alliance Air flights connecting Ambikapur to Delhi and Kolkata, with the aim of expanding air connectivity and making air travel accessible to common people. The two new flights will be operated by Alliance Air every week from Ambikapur's Maa Mahamaya Airport, with services to Delhi on Mondays and Wednesdays and Kolkata on Thursdays and Saturdays. This is part of a larger initiative by the state government to expand aviation services across Chhattisgarh under the Centre's Regional Connectivity Scheme (UDAN).

political_articles_data.story_summary: Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai has virtually launched flight services from Ambikapur to Delhi and Kolkata, marking the state's expansion of aviation services. The two Alliance Air flights will operate weekly, reducing travel time significantly. The new service aims to boost trade, tourism, and economic activities in the region, connecting Ambikapur with major markets and investment opportunities.

Nitish Kumar has resigned as an MLC in the state legislature, fulfilling a constitutional requirement after being elected to the Rajya Sabha. Despite this, he remains the Chief Minister of Bihar for now due to the Constitution allowing him to serve in that role without being a member of the state legislature. The BJP is still undecided on who will succeed Nitish as CM.

The BJP is yet to reach a consensus on who will succeed him as CM once Nitish takes oath as a Rajya Sabha member on April 10.

input_article.title: Naxalism has been almost eradicated from Bastar: Amit Shah in Lok Sabha
input_article.summary:
* Union Home Minister Amit Shah stated that Naxalism has been almost eradicated from Bastar, a region in India, and is now on the path of progress.
* Shah attributed the decline of Naxalism to development initiatives launched by his government, including the establishment of schools, ration shops, primary health centers, and Aadhaar cards in every village across Bastar.
* The government has set a target to eliminate Naxalism nationwide by March 31 this year.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah stated that Naxalism has been almost eradicated from Bastar, Chhattisgarh, with the region transitioning to progress. The government has launched campaigns to establish schools, open ration shops, and establish healthcare centers, providing Aadhaar cards and food grains to local residents. Shah criticized Congress for decades of systemic neglect and accused them of marginalizing tribal communities during their rule. The development is seen as a significant step towards eradicating Naxalism in India.

input_article.title: Naveen Patnaik slams Nishikant Dubey’s remarks on Biju Patnaik, calls them ‘outrageous’
input_article.summary: BJP MP Nishikant Dubey recently made remarks questioning Biju Patnaik's role during the Sino-Indian War of 1962, claiming that Jawaharlal Nehru had US assistance and coordination. Odisha BJD president Naveen Patnaik criticized these comments as "outrageous" and baseless, describing them as false insinuations about his father's role in national defense. Dubey denied making any allegations against Patnaik.

political_articles_data.story_summary: BJP MP Nishikant Dubey has faced criticism from Biju Janata Dal (BJD) president Naveen Patnaik over his remarks on former Odisha chief minister Biju Patnaik, with Patnaik calling them "outrageous" and lacking an understanding of history. Dubey had questioned Biju Patnaik's role during the 1962 Sino-Indian War, alleging that then Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru conducted the war effort with American assistance. The controversy has led to BJD leaders stepping up protests, including Rajya Sabha MP Sasmit Patra resigning from a parliamentary committee in protest against Dubey's comments.

input_article.summary: The Biju Janata Dal (BJD) walked out of the Rajya Sabha due to remarks made by Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) lawmaker Nishikant Dubey about party founder Biju Patnaik. BJD's Sasmit Patra had resigned from a parliamentary committee in protest against the disparaging remarks on Sunday. Dubey claimed he did not make any allegations against Patnaik, but expressed regret if his tweets caused harm to others.

The BJP denied making any allegations against Patnaik, while the BJD termed the comments as "disrespectful" and accused the ruling party of lowering parliamentary discourse. A BJD MP resigned from a parliamentary panel in protest, further escalating tensions. The walkout comes after Dubey's previous remarks were seen as disparaging by the opposition.

input_article.title: BJP president Nitin Nabin resigns as Bihar MLC after election to Rajya Sabha
input_article.summary: Nitin Nabin, the president of the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), has resigned as a member of the Bihar legislative council after being elected to the Rajya Sabha. Nabin must vacate his seat in the state legislature within 14 days of election to the upper house, as per the 'Prohibition of Simultaneous Membership Rules, 1950'. He was first elected to the Bihar legislative council in 2006 after a bypoll following the death of his father.

The resignations were submitted to respective party leaders and legislative council chairs. Both Kumar and Nabin will now focus on their new roles in the Rajya Sabha.

input_article.title: Shabir Ahmed Lone, Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorist from Jammu and Kashmir, arrested by Delhi Police
input_article.summary: Shabir Ahmed Lone, a Lashkar-e-Taiba terrorist from Jammu and Kashmir, was arrested by the Delhi Police over a month after pro-Pakistan and pro-terror posters were found at Janpath Metro Station. The posters glorified slain terrorist Burhan Wani and carried anti-India slogans. Lone is believed to be the head of a module of "highly radicalised operatives" who defaced metro stations in Delhi and planned further terror-based activities.

political_articles_data.story_summary: The Delhi Police Special Cell has arrested Shabir Ahmed Lone, a senior Lashkar-e-Taiba commander and one of India's most wanted terrorists. He was accused of leading a module of "highly radicalised operatives" who defaced metro stations and planned terror-based activities in Delhi. Authorities said he had established an operational base in Kolkata and directed reconnaissance of Hindu temples in Delhi, using Bangladeshi nationals to evade detection. This is his third known encounter with Indian law enforcement.

related_reports.title: Lashkar-e-Taiba's Shabir Ahmed Lone arrested: How he built terror network from Kolkata, targeted Delhi temples - Mint
related_reports.description: Lashkar-e-Taiba's Shabir Ahmed Lone arrested: How he built terror network from Kolkata, targeted Delhi temples  Mint
related_reports.summary: The Delhi Police special cell has arrested Shabir Ahmed Lone, a senior Lashkar-e-Taiba commander considered one of India's most wanted terrorists. Lone was allegedly operating an operational base in Kolkata and directed reconnaissance of prominent Hindu temples in Delhi as part of a cross-border terror network. He was apprehended on Sunday night after police tracked his movements from Nepal and intercepted him near Ghazipur, seizing his mobile handset and foreign currencies at the scene.

input_article.title: Can Pinarayi Vijayan sail through or will the ‘alternating power’ curse make a comeback in Kerala?
input_article.summary:
* The opposition Congress-led UDF is challenging Pinarayi Vijayan's leadership by backing four CPI(M) candidates who have filed nominations as Independents.
* Pinarayi Vijayan, the chief minister of Kerala, has dropped his two-term limit rule and will contest the elections from a new constituency, amidst concerns about anti-incumbency and rebellion within the CPI(M).
* The 2021 election saw the CPI(M) break the alternating power curse, but this time they face a similar challenge as the UDF backs rebels in key strongholds.

political_articles_data.story_summary: Kerala Assembly polls 2026 are underway, pitting Pinarayi Vijayan's CPI(M)-led LDF against Congress-led UDF, with BJP-led NDA hoping to make an impact. The opposition has backed four CPI(M) candidates as Independents, posing a challenge for the ruling party. As campaigning reaches its fever pitch, conspiracy theories and identity politics are dominating the electioneering, with prominent leaders including Modi and Rahul Gandhi making appearances.

He claimed that the BJP-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) is the sole "A-team" in the upcoming assembly polls. The LDF and UDF, according to Modi, have a long-standing understanding to share power and resources, with the state currently facing a public debt of ₹5 lakh crore.

Modi alleges that the LDF and UDF have prioritized electoral gains over governance and are beholden to special interests. The BJP aims to deliver rapid development to Kerala, with a "developed Kerala" as its guarantee.

input_article.title: Nitish Kumar ‘may resign’ from Bihar assembly today
input_article.summary: Nitish Kumar, Bihar's chief minister, may resign as an MLC (member of legislative council) by March 30 due to conflicting roles. He was elected to the Rajya Sabha on March 16 and must resign from his state legislature seat within 14 days. Nitish is expected to relinquish both his position as MLC and potentially as chief minister, although a confirmation has not been made.

political_articles_data.story_summary:
Here's a unified news summary in 3-4 lines combining all sources:

Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar is likely to resign from the state legislative council on March 30, as per constitutional rules after being elected to the Rajya Sabha unopposed on March 16. BJP national president Nitin Nabin also faces a deadline of resignation by March 30 due to simultaneous membership in both Parliament and state legislature. Both may choose to quit on Ramnavami (March 27) or 28, as these are the only available dates within the 14-day stipulated period. The decision comes amidst deliberations about potential replacements for Nitish Kumar, who will continue to serve as chief minister without being a member of the state legislature.

Nitish can continue as Chief Minister without being a member of the state legislature, whereas Nitin must vacate one of his seats in the legislature. The next chief minister could be from the BJP, with Deputy Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary and other leaders considered probable names for the position.

The move was formalised when JD(U) MLC Sanjay Gandhi submitted his resignation letter. This development follows the Prohibition of Simultaneous Membership Rules, which requires an individual to relinquish one position within 14 days of being elected to Parliament.

input_article.title: Telangana to cut salary of those who do not care for their parents
input_article.summary: The Telangana Assembly has passed a new bill requiring public representatives, government employees, and private employees to care for their elderly parents. Failure to comply will result in a deduction of 15% of salary or ₹10,000, whichever is lower. The bill aims to ensure dignity for senior citizens and provides a formal grievance redressal mechanism.

political_articles_data.story_summary: The Telangana Assembly has passed the 'Telangana Employees Accountability and Monitoring of Parental Support Bill, 2026,' which mandates that public representatives, government employees, and private employees care for their elderly parents. Failure to comply will result in a deduction of 15% of salary or ₹10,000, whichever is lower, payable to the parents. The bill aims to ensure dignity for senior citizens and go beyond existing central legislation. It covers more individuals than previous laws, including public representatives.

input_article.summary:

* The Central Guerrilla Headquarters of the CPI(Maoist) was active for over four decades but has now been largely dismantled, with no meaningful military capability left.
* Anti-Naxal operations will continue with retraining and reorientation of special forces, including the DRG and Bastar Fighters, to focus on policing and law enforcement.

political_articles_data.story_summary: India's anti-Maoist operation has made significant progress, with Naxal forces dwindling to almost zero cadres, expected to surrender as operations continue. The Central Guerrilla Headquarters was dismantled, and the military strength of the CPI(Maoist) has shrunk from over 4,000 cadres to double or single digits. The Indian government's goal of eliminating Maoism by March 2026 is nearing completion, with ongoing security forces gradually reoriented for policing operations.

input_article.title: Niti Aayog to revamp Aspirational Districts Programme
input_article.summary: The NITI Aayog plans to revamp the Aspirational Districts Programme (ADP), aiming to incorporate major refinements and structural improvements. The revamped programme, known as ADP 2.0, will feature revised key performance indicators, an emphasis on innovation and training people in selected districts, and potentially new districts being added to the scheme. The top-performing districts among the original 112 ADP districts may be upgraded to "Inspirational Districts" category and excluded from award funds under the challenge method.

The program may add new districts, revise key performance indicators, and focus on innovation and training in selected areas. The cash rewards for top performers might be eliminated due to saturated indicators, with a shift towards outcome-based assessments instead.

input_article.title: Centre likely to extend SIM-binding timeline for WhatsApp and other messaging platforms
input_article.summary: The Indian Department of Telecommunications (DoT) plans to extend the deadline for messaging apps to comply with SIM binding requirements. The initial compliance deadline was set for February 2026, but due to technical challenges faced by companies, it has been extended to December-end. Apple is reportedly working on a solution to address technical constraints related to iOS, while Meta (WhatsApp's parent company) and Zoho are also engaging with the DoT to find feasible solutions.

political_articles_data.story_summary:
Here's a unified news summary:

The Indian government may extend the deadline for messaging apps to comply with its SIM-binding directive due to technical challenges faced by companies. The original deadline was set for February 2026 but has been missed. Industry players have cited system-level challenges, coordination issues with operating system providers, and structural changes required to meet the requirements. The Department of Telecommunications (DoT) is now expected to extend the compliance deadline to December 2026, allowing companies like WhatsApp and Signal to address technical constraints before meeting the requirement.

input_article.title: WTO talks on e-commerce duties moratorium deadlocked as reform plan nears deal
input_article.summary:
* Talks on extending the e-commerce duties moratorium due to expire this month have become deadlocked, with differences between the US and India over its duration remaining unresolved. 
* A new draft document proposes extending negotiations by four years plus an additional buffer year to 2031, but Brazil has expressed objections.
* A wider WTO reform plan is nearing a deal, including efforts to improve decision-making in a consensus-based system and extend trade benefits to developing countries.

political_articles_data.story_summary: WTO talks on extending the e-commerce moratorium, set to expire this month, are stalled due to disagreements between countries, particularly the US and India. A draft reform plan, aimed at improving decision-making and trade benefits for developing countries, is nearing an agreement despite initial resistance. Diplomats report that Brazil's objections have blocked a four-year extension, while the US wants a permanent one-year extension. The WTO's relevance is being tested amid ongoing tariff-fueled trade turmoil.

input_article.title: 'Swift support': Sri Lanka receives 38,000 tonnes of fuel from India amid West Asia crisis
input_article.summary: Sri Lanka has received 38,000 metric tonnes of fuel from India as part of a "neighbourhood policy" response to the West Asia crisis. The fuel shipment arrived in Colombo on Sunday, following disruptions caused by the conflict between US-Israel and Iran. Sri Lanka had secured supplies from other countries before turning to India for support due to the issues with its existing contracts.

political_articles_data.story_summary:
Here's a unified news summary in 3-4 lines combining all sources:

Sri Lanka has received 38,000 metric tonnes of fuel from India amid the West Asia conflict, which has caused disruptions to global oil and gas supply chains. The fuel shipment, delivered on March 28, includes 20,000 MT of diesel and 18,000 MT of petrol, and was made possible through India's "Neighbourhood First" policy. Sri Lankan President Anura Kumara Dissanayake thanked Indian leaders for their swift support in addressing the island nation's energy crisis. The fuel assistance is part of India's efforts to stabilize regional security and ensure uninterrupted fuel supplies worldwide.

input_article.title: Repeat drug offenders can be declared ‘goonda’, says HC
input_article.summary: A Kerala High Court bench has ruled that someone can be declared a "goonda" under the Kerala Anti-Social Activities Act if apprehended with drugs and psychotropic substances more than once. The court held that possession of small quantities of drugs does not automatically render an accused a "drug offender". The ruling aims to curb recidivism in drug abuse cases.

non_political_articles_data.story_summary:
Here's a unified news summary in 3-4 lines combining all sources:

A Kerala High Court bench has clarified that possessing even a small quantity of narcotic drugs can be considered "stocking" under the Anti-Social Activities (Prevention) Act, making an individual liable as a "drug offender" or "goonda". The court's decision aims to combat the growing problem of drug abuse in the state, which poses serious challenges to families and society. It seeks to subject first-time offenders to rehabilitation programs and zero tolerance approach to tackle recidivism tendencies.

related_reports.title: Possessing even small quantity of narcotic drugs can attract Kaapa provisions: Kerala HC - The Times of India
related_reports.description: Possessing even small quantity of narcotic drugs can attract Kaapa provisions: Kerala HC  The Times of India
related_reports.summary:
Here is a 3-line factual summary of the news article:

A Kerala High Court decision clarifies that possessing a small quantity of narcotics constitutes "stocking" and qualifies as an "anti-social activity" under the Kerala Anti-Social Activities (Prevention) Act, 2007. The court rejected a previous ruling suggesting that only commercial activities related to sale or distribution fall within this provision. Possession for personal consumption also now qualifies as an "anti-social activity", according to the court's interpretation of the law.

input_article.summary: The Indian Oil Marketing Corporation (OMC) increased the price of commercial liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cylinders by more than 10% in major metro cities, while aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices were more than doubled for international flights. The government defended the hikes, citing OMCs' losses due to rising global oil prices. ATF rates for domestic scheduled carriers were increased by less than 9%.
